target_stack: green-ai (ASP.NET Core 9 + Blazor Server + SQL Server + In-Memory Cache)
rationale: The localization domain is infrastructure-level utility. It has no complex business rules, no domain events, and no external service dependencies beyond the DB and in-memory cache. The current architecture is sound — the primary issues are gaps (GAP_004, GAP_005) not structural problems.

api_surface.recommendation: Keep DB-backed translations. Expose via a dedicated LocalizationEndpoints module rather than mixing into CommonController.

public_endpoint.description: Returns flat JSON dictionary of UPPER(ResourceName) → ResourceValue for a language. Used by frontend on bootstrap.
public_endpoint.green_ai_equivalent: ILocalizationService.GetAll(languageId) already returns this. Wire it up.

routes: POST /api/localization/search — paginated search (replace current mixed query/filter approach)
POST /api/localization — create new key (auto-creates all language placeholders)

database.recommendation: Keep dbo.LocaleStringResources and dbo.Languages as-is. Remove dbo.Countries (GAP_003).
schema_changes: No changes to Languages table — all 6 languages including German.
No changes to LocaleStringResources table — indexes are correct.
Consider adding DATETIME2 LastModifiedUtc column to LocaleStringResources for change tracking.
Consider adding soft-delete (IsDeleted bit) to enable auditing of deleted keys.
Countries table: drop or archive (GAP_003 decision needed first).
database.migration_note: No breaking migrations needed for core localization functionality.

caching.recommendation: Keep per-key caching for GetLocalizedResource(key, langId). Fix GAP_002 — add cache invalidation to batch import.
caching.green_ai_pattern: IMemoryCache or IDistributedCache. Current 'sms.lsr.*' pattern-clear approach is acceptable.
caching.improvement: Add ETag or Last-Modified header to GET /api/localization/translations so browsers and CDNs can cache effectively. Current response has no cache headers.

frontend.platform: Blazor Server — no Angular, no ngx-translate
frontend.translation_pattern: Use Microsoft.Extensions.Localization or a thin wrapper over ILocalizationService directly in Blazor components.
frontend.key_design: Keep UPPER-cased dot-notation keys (e.g. 'SHARED.DATEFORMATSHORT') — consistent with existing DB content.
frontend.language_switching: Blazor CultureInfo.CurrentUICulture — set from user profile LanguageId on login. No client-side translation loading needed (server renders).

admin_ui.description: Rebuild translation-management.component.ts as a Blazor page.
features: Language selector (all 6 including German — fix GAP_004)
admin_ui.note: Server-side pagination preferred — DB can handle the filtering natively rather than loading all rows in memory as current implementation does.

gaps_to_fix_during_rebuild.action: Add cache invalidation to ImportLocaleStringResourcesAsync. One line: _cacheManager.RemoveByPattern('sms.lsr.').
Decision required: drop Countries table or migrate mappings from CountryConstants.cs into it. Rebuild is the right time to resolve this.
Add German (DE=6) to all frontend language enumerations and translation loading. In green-ai/Blazor this is automatic if CultureInfo is configured correctly — no separate enum needed.
Non-issue in Blazor — server renders translations, no client-side cache update needed.

effort_estimate.backend_port: LOW — ILocalizationService interface is clean. Wire new endpoints, fix cache invalidation.
effort_estimate.database_migration: LOW — no schema changes required for core functionality.
effort_estimate.frontend_rebuild: MEDIUM — Blazor admin page is straightforward but requires server-side pagination refactor (current code loads all rows in memory).
effort_estimate.total_estimate: 2–3 dev-days for full rebuild including German fix and server-side pagination.
